notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateOccCapacityRequest, GetOccCapacityRequest, ListOccCapacityRequests, UpdateOccCapacityRequest, and DeleteOccCapacityRequest are present in v65.110.0. The SDK also exposes ListOccCapacityRequestsInternal and UpdateInternalOccCapacityRequest, but those internal helpers are outside the published provider-backed surface.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: lifecycle states are CREATING, UPDATING, ACTIVE, DELETING, DELETED, and FAILED. Create, update, and delete responses expose retry-after headers; create and update also return the OccCapacityRequest body.

Pinned provider coverage: the provider-facts inventory aligned to formal/sources.lock exposes resource oci_capacity_management_occ_capacity_request plus singular and list datasources oci_capacity_management_occ_capacity_request and oci_capacity_management_occ_capacity_requests for this kind. The checked-in mutability docs fixture is used because the live Terraform Registry page currently renders as a JavaScript shell instead of a deterministic argument-reference HTML page.

Repo-authored runtime intentionally does not adopt untracked live capacity requests by list lookup. DisplayName is mutable, the public list surface cannot prove full create-time equivalence for region/date/details, and the internal list requires occCustomerGroupId that the CR does not model.

Repo-authored mutation policy matches the public update surface: only displayName, requestState, freeformTags, and definedTags reconcile in place. All other meaningful spec fields remain create-time only drift for the published runtime.
Delete confirmation stays lifecycle-based rather than header-only: the finalizer remains until DeleteOccCapacityRequest succeeds and GetOccCapacityRequest confirms DELETED or NotFound.
